intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Perfect. Now I'll execute the workflow on the GSM5746268 sample using the recipe as a guide:

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import os

# Set figure output to working directory
sc.settings.figdir = '.'

# Load the 10x triplet for GSM5746268
D = os.environ['DATA_DIR']
pre = "GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1"

# Read the three files explicitly (GEO loose triplet layout)
adata = sc.read_mtx(f"{D}/{pre}.matrix.mtx.gz").T  # mtx is genes × cells → transpose
adata.obs_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
adata.var_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values  # col 2 = symbols
adata.var_names_make_unique()

print(f"Loaded {adata.n_obs} cells × {adata.n_vars} genes")
print(f"First 5 barcodes: {list(adata.obs_names[:5])}")
print(f"First 5 genes: {list(adata.var_names[:5])}")
print(f"Matrix shape: {adata.X.shape}")
print(f"Data type: {type(adata.X)}, sparse: {type(adata.X).__name__}")
